full_prompt: Design the primary enclosure of a sounding-balloon stratospheric payload entered in the Technion Aerospace Faculty student balloon challenge. The payload rides under a latex sounding balloon with a burst altitude of approximately 30 km AGL and descends under a 0.9 m parachute at a terminal rate of 6 m/s at sea level. Enclosure envelope: 200 x 150 x 150 mm, total payload wet mass 1.2 kg (including batteries, camera, radio, sensors). Housing is 3D-printed PETG (E 2.1 GPa, tensile 45 MPa at 20 C, but tensile drops to 40 MPa at -40 C and glass transition Tg = 85 C; density 1270 kg/m^3). The enclosure walls are 3 mm thick with internal reinforcing ribs on all six faces. Stratospheric environment: ambient pressure 1 kPa absolute at 30 km, ambient temperature -60 C, solar irradiance 1360 W/m^2 on the sun-exposed face (no atmospheric attenuation at altitude), internal dissipation 5 W (radio + compute). Parachute attachment is a 6 mm M6 eyelet at the top face. Load cases: (LC1) launch handling / drop during release: 5 g on 1.2 kg in any direction, applied at the parachute eyelet (equivalent 60 N tensile); (LC2) parachute opening shock: 150 N at the eyelet axial; (LC3) burst shock at balloon release: 3 g impulse on 1.2 kg at CoM; (LC4) ground impact at 6 m/s: 10 g equivalent deceleration on 1.2 kg onto the bottom face; (LC5) steady stratospheric thermal at 30 km cruise: -60 C ambient, 1360 W/m^2 solar on top face, 5 W internal, free convection negligible at 1 kPa (radiation + conduction dominate). Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1-LC4 at -40 C (worst cold-soak before ground impact), max enclosure stress must remain below 20 MPa (40 / 2.0 FoS at cold); (R2) under LC5 steady thermal, internal air temperature must remain above 0 C (to keep electronics and batteries operable) AND enclosure max surface temperature must remain below 65 C (Tg - 20 C margin) on the sun-exposed face; (R3) eyelet pull-out reinforcement must carry LC2 parachute shock without local stress exceeding 20 MPa in PETG near the insert; (R4) enclosure dry mass (walls + ribs only) must not exceed 250 g; (R5) enclosure must remain vacuum-sealed (no active venting) - verify by showing differential pressure case: internal 101 kPa vs external 1 kPa produces an effective outward pressure of 100 kPa on all walls; check wall deflection below 2 mm (to avoid structural failure or hinge damage). Fluid, radiation (ionizing), and buckling analyses not primary gates. Deliverables: STEP AP242 solid model, rib schedule, mechanical FEA at room and cold temperatures, steady-state thermal with solar + radiation BCs, pressure-differential FEA, mass budget.
short_prompt:
Design the primary enclosure of a Technion Aerospace Faculty sounding-balloon stratospheric payload. Ride under a latex balloon to ~30 km AGL burst, descend under a 0.9 m parachute at 6 m/s terminal at sea level. Payload wet 1.2 kg; 3D-printed PETG walls 3 mm with internal ribs on all six faces.

Envelope: 200 x 150 x 150 mm box with 6 mm M6 parachute eyelet at top. Stratospheric environment: 1 kPa absolute at 30 km, -60 C ambient, 1360 W/m^2 solar on the sun-exposed face, 5 W internal dissipation.

Factor regime: material FoS 2.0 at -40 C cold-soak (allowable 20 MPa); electronics-operability thermal gate (internal air >= 0 C and enclosure surface <= 65 C = Tg - 20); local-insert stress at eyelet < 20 MPa; pressure-differential wall deflection < 2 mm; dry mass <= 250 g.

Load cases.
- Launch / drop handling: 5 g on 1.2 kg (60 N tensile) at the eyelet in any direction.
- Parachute-opening shock: 150 N axial at the eyelet.
- Burst-release impulse: 3 g on 1.2 kg at CoM.
- Ground impact at 6 m/s: 10 g equivalent decel on 1.2 kg onto the bottom face.
- Steady stratospheric thermal at 30 km cruise (solar + radiation + conduction; convection negligible at 1 kPa).
- Pressure differential: sealed internal 101 kPa vs external 1 kPa = 100 kPa outward on all walls.

prompt.title: Technion Sounding-Balloon Stratospheric Payload Enclosure
prompt.brief: PETG enclosure 200x150x150 mm for 1.2 kg payload at 30 km, -60 C / 1360 W/m^2 / 100 kPa pressure differential.

load_cases.name: steady_stratospheric_thermal
load_cases.application: -60 C + solar + 5 W internal at 1 kPa

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: solid TET10 for ribbed wall; shell for skins
analysis_types: steady thermal with solar + internal heat

notes.needs_rulebook_fetch_reason: Technion balloon/CanSat rulebook is an internal-university document with no public PDF surfaced. Numeric gates extrapolate from ESA BEXUS and Technion space-lab known practice.

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Thin atmosphere; free convection neglected at 1 kPa.
exclusions_explained.radiation_analysis: Solar input as surface flux; ionizing radiation not evaluated at this altitude.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Ribbed enclosure; stress gate bounds stability.
